pattern: Column-independent DP: for each column pick a digit 0-9 to assign to all its cells; transitions enforce adjacent columns differ, and the cost is the number of cells not already equal to the chosen digit.
hints: Each column must be uniform (all cells the same value 0-9); count the frequency of each digit in every column to compute assignment cost.
The problem decomposes into choosing one digit per column such that adjacent columns have different digits; use DP over columns with 10 states.
DP state: dp[j][d] = minimum operations to assign digits to columns 0..j where column j is assigned digit d. Transition: dp[j][d] = min over d2 != d of dp[j-1][d2] + cost(j, d).

edge_inputs.description: single cell grid already satisfying conditions
1x2 grid with same values — must change one column
all zeros — just need adjacent columns different
each column already uniform and different
